---
title: "Reuse Your Shiny Module"
---

## The payoff

You already have `filter_panel(input, output, session, data, columns)` from
the last section. It doesn't know anything about `tips` specifically — it
takes *any* dataframe and *any* column list.

## A second dataset, zero new filter code

`palmerpenguins` — the dataset from your very first app — has a categorical
column (`species`) and two numeric ones (`bill_length_mm`, `body_mass_g`).
Exactly the shape our module already handles.

```{.python}
penguins_filter = filter_panel(
    "penguins_filter",
    data=penguins,
    columns=["species", "bill_length_mm", "body_mass_g"],
)
```

## Two tabs, two datasets, one module

```{.python}
with ui.navset_card_tab():
    with ui.nav_panel("Tips"):
        with ui.layout_sidebar():
            with ui.sidebar():
                tips_filter = filter_panel(
                    "tips_filter", data=tips, columns=["total_bill", "time"]
                )
            ...

    with ui.nav_panel("Penguins"):
        with ui.layout_sidebar():
            with ui.sidebar():
                penguins_filter = filter_panel(
                    "penguins_filter", data=penguins,
                    columns=["species", "bill_length_mm", "body_mass_g"],
                )
            ...
```

Two different namespace ids (`"tips_filter"`, `"penguins_filter"`) keep the
two module instances' inputs from colliding.

## The composed app

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/demos/app-07-modules_compose-express.py >}}

## file: filter_module_express.py
{{< include /../code/demos/filter_module_express.py >}}
```

## Recap

- Modules are namespaced functions — write the logic once
- Reuse across datasets, across tabs, across apps
- Same module works from Shiny Core *or* Shiny Express

## Exercise

See [Exercise 7](../exercises.qmd#ex7).
